Land Rover Range Rover P400e Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Range Rover P400e engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Range Rover P400e engine typically costs £6,500–£11,000 supply only. Fitting adds around £1,500–£2,500 depending on location and workshop rates. A brand-new OEM engine costs approximately £20,000–£22,000 installed. Hybrid battery replacement is a separate cost — typically £6,000–£10,000+ from a main dealer.

mainEntity.name: Is the Range Rover P400e engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The P400e uses the AJ20P6 2.0-litre four-cylinder Ingenium petrol engine, paired with a 105kW electric motor. The engine has documented issues: charge air cooler coolant leaks (JLR is investigating these), misfire/hesitation, and thermostat performance problems. The hybrid system adds complexity, but the petrol engine itself is more reliable than the diesel Ingenium units.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Range Rover P400e?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) Charge air cooler coolant leaks — coolant escapes at high revs and burns off through the exhaust, (2) Engine misfire / hesitation — rough idle and hesitation during acceleration, and (3) Thermostat performance issues — DTC P0128-00 flagged.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover P400e engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £40,000–£70,000 Range Rover P400e, a £6,500–£11,000 recon is around 10–30% of the car's value — worth it given Range Rover values remain strong. However, if the hybrid battery also needs replacement (£6,000–£10,000+), evaluate total repair costs against the vehicle's value.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Range Rover P400e engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The P400e uses the AJ20P6 (also known as PT204) — a 2.0-litre four-cylinder Ingenium petrol engine. The engine code is stamped on the cylinder block. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the P400e coolant leak, or does it need engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — coolant leaks on the P400e are almost always repairable without engine replacement. The most common fix is charge air cooler replacement (£800–£1,500). The key is catching the leak early — ignored coolant loss can lead to overheating and catastrophic engine damage. Regular coolant level checks are essential.

eraMap.title: Land Rover Range Rover P400e — Which Powertrain Does Your Car Actually Have?

Revised L460 model — same powertrain, similar risk profile. Thermostat and CVVL system issues documented

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Range Rover P400e Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Range Rover P400e engine typically takes 15–25 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). Hybrid system calibration and ECU programming add additional labour.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover P400e engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Charge Air Cooler Coolant Leak
problems.symptoms: Low coolant warning light, coolant level dropping with no visible external leak, no white smoke from exhaust despite significant coolant loss, coolant loss more pronounced at high revs
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — reported across low and high-mileage examples
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant level drops frequently

problems.recommendation: JLR is investigating coolant level low complaints on Ingenium I4 2.0L petrol engines. The root cause is often an internal leak in the Charge Air Cooler (CAC). Coolant escapes at high revs, burns off through the exhaust without noticeable smoke, and the expansion tank level drops. The leak can be identified by pressurising the Charge Air Cooler component specifically. If you're topping up coolant regularly, book a pressure test immediately. A £800–£1,500 charge air cooler replacement is far cheaper than the overheating damage that follows if ignored.

failureLink.label: Read the full coolant leak guide → /failures/charge-air-cooler-coolant-leak/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Engine Misfire / Hesitation & Rough Idle
problems.symptoms: Check Engine Light (MIL) illuminated, engine running rough, hesitation when accelerating, engine speed instability at low RPM, rough idle, vehicle may stall and restart on next ignition cycle
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — reported on 2019 MY-onward vehicles
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if symptoms appear

problems.recommendation: NHTSA documents confirm misfire or hesitation complaints on 19MY-onward Range Rover and Range Rover Sport equipped with the AJ20P6 engine. A revised PCM and TCM calibration is available to address engine speed instability at low RPM. In some cases, the Continuous Variable Valve Lift (CVVL) system may be at fault, requiring CVVL unit replacement. If you experience rough idle or hesitation, have the dealer check for available software updates first — this is often a straightforward fix.

failureLink.label: Read the full misfire / hesitation guide → /failures/ingenium-misfire-hesitation/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Thermostat Performance Issue (DTC P0128-00)
problems.symptoms: Check Engine Light, thermostat range performance issue flagged, potential cold-start running problems
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — documented on AJ20P6-equipped vehicles

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if warning light appears

problems.recommendation: JLR has identified a thermostat range performance issue on AJ20-P6 engine vehicles, flagged by DTC P0128-00. The fix is to replace the thermostat assembly as per Topix and clear the DTC. This is a relatively straightforward repair. If you see a check engine light, have the codes read — if P0128-00 is present, thermostat replacement is the recommended course of action.

failureLink.label: Read the full thermostat failure guide → /failures/thermostat-performance-issue/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover P400e — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Often warranty-covered; assess cost vs vehicle value

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Range Rover P400e? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with JLR-approved oil and coolant specs
* No history of low coolant warnings or frequent top-ups
* Evidence of regular use (hybrid batteries degrade with extended inactivity)
* Remaining manufacturer warranty — the P400e is complex and benefits from cover
avoidIf: * Low coolant warning light or frequent top-ups without documented fix
* Check engine light with no diagnosis — hybrid systems are complex
* No history of the 2.0-litre Ingenium petrol's known issues being addressed
* Long periods of inactivity — hybrid battery health is a concern

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Range Rover P400e is a sophisticated plug-in hybrid that pairs a 2.0-litre Ingenium petrol engine with a 105kW electric motor. It delivers impressive performance (404 bhp, 640 Nm) and up to 30+ miles of electric-only range. However, the 2.0-litre Ingenium petrol engine has documented issues: charge air cooler coolant leaks, misfire/hesitation, and thermostat performance problems. Buy with documented service history, ideally with remaining manufacturer warranty, and you'll have a genuinely capable and efficient hybrid luxury SUV.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover P400e engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover P400e (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Coolant Level — Check for frequent top-ups; this is the most common P400e issue
* Cold Start — Listen for rough idle or hesitation when the engine first runs
* Check Engine Light — Any warning lights? Have codes been read? Look for P0128-00
* Service History — Including the 2.0-litre Ingenium petrol (AJ20P6) and confirmed coolant system inspections
* Hybrid Battery Health — Check electric-only range matches claimed figures (30+ miles)
* Charging System — Test both AC and DC charging functionality
* Warranty Status — Manufacturer warranty is valuable on these complex vehicles
* Drivability — Test for hesitation during acceleration and low-speed running
